frontend.title: What is the Virtual DOM and how does React's reconciliation algorithm work?
frontend.hint: Explain diffing algorithm, fiber architecture, and why it's faster than direct DOM manipulation.

frontend.title: Explain CSS specificity and how the cascade algorithm resolves conflicts.
frontend.hint: Inline > ID > Class > Element. Mention !important and its risks.

frontend.title: What is the difference between useCallback and useMemo? When do you actually need them?
frontend.hint: useMemo caches values, useCallback caches functions. Only use when computation is expensive or referential equality matters.

frontend.title: How does JavaScript event loop work? Explain microtasks vs macrotasks.
frontend.hint: Call stack → Microtask queue (Promises) → Macrotask queue (setTimeout). Microtasks drain completely before macrotasks.

frontend.title: What are Web Workers and when would you use them?
frontend.hint: Background threads for CPU-intensive tasks. Cannot access DOM. Use for large data processing.

frontend.title: Explain the difference between server-side rendering, static site generation, and CSR.
frontend.hint: SSR: per-request HTML. SSG: build-time HTML. CSR: JS renders in browser. Each has different TTFB, SEO, and caching tradeoffs.

frontend.title: How would you optimize a React app that renders a list of 10,000 items?
frontend.hint: Virtual scrolling (react-window), pagination, memoization, windowing. Never render all items at once.

frontend.title: What is CORS and how do you handle it in a frontend application?
frontend.hint: Same-origin policy restriction. Handle via server headers, proxy, or JSONP for legacy.

frontend.title: Explain CSS Grid vs Flexbox — when do you use each?
frontend.hint: Flexbox: 1D layout (row or column). Grid: 2D layout. Use Grid for page layouts, Flexbox for components.

frontend.title: What is code splitting and how do you implement it in Next.js?
frontend.hint: dynamic() imports, React.lazy + Suspense. Split by route automatically in Next.js, manually for heavy components.

frontend.title: How do you handle authentication state in a Next.js App Router application?
frontend.hint: Server-side session cookies, middleware for route protection, client-side state sync with cookies.

frontend.title: What is TypeScript's structural typing? How is it different from nominal typing?
frontend.hint: TypeScript checks shape, not name. Two types are compatible if they have the same structure, regardless of their declared name.

frontend.title: Explain how CSS `position: sticky` works and common pitfalls.
frontend.hint: Positioned relative until scroll threshold, then fixed. Pitfall: parent overflow hidden breaks it.

frontend.title: What is a closure in JavaScript? Give a real-world use case.
frontend.hint: A function that remembers its outer scope. Used in event handlers, memoization, module pattern, factory functions.

frontend.title: How does lazy loading improve performance? How do you implement it for images?
frontend.hint: loading='lazy' attribute, Intersection Observer API, Next.js Image component handles this automatically.

backend.title: How would you design a RESTful API for a social media platform? What endpoints would you create?
backend.hint: Resource-based URLs, proper HTTP methods, versioning, pagination, error codes.

backend.title: Explain database indexing. When should you NOT use an index?
backend.hint: Speeds up reads, slows down writes. Avoid on small tables, frequently updated columns, low-cardinality columns.

backend.title: What is the N+1 query problem and how do you solve it?
backend.hint: 1 query to fetch list + N queries for each item's relations. Solve with JOIN, eager loading, DataLoader.

backend.title: Explain ACID properties in databases. When would you sacrifice some for performance?
backend.hint: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability. NoSQL often trades ACID for availability/performance (BASE model).

backend.title: How do JWTs work? What are their security implications?
backend.hint: Header.Payload.Signature. Stateless but cannot be invalidated. Use short expiry + refresh tokens. Never store sensitive data in payload.

backend.title: What is database sharding? When would you implement it?
backend.hint: Horizontal partitioning across multiple servers. Use when single-node can't handle load. Challenges: cross-shard queries, rebalancing.

backend.title: Explain the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases. When do you choose each?
backend.hint: SQL: structured, ACID, relations. NoSQL: flexible schema, horizontal scale, varied data models. Choose based on data structure and scale needs.

backend.title: How does connection pooling work and why is it important?
backend.hint: Reuses DB connections instead of creating new ones. Avoids overhead. Set appropriate pool size based on DB and server capacity.

backend.title: What is GraphQL? When is it better than REST?
backend.hint: Client specifies exact data needed. Solves over/under-fetching. Better for complex, nested data. REST better for simple CRUD.

backend.title: How would you implement rate limiting for an API?
backend.hint: Token bucket or sliding window algorithm. Use Redis for distributed rate limiting. Return 429 with Retry-After header.

backend.title: Explain event-driven architecture. What are its trade-offs?
backend.hint: Services communicate via events (Kafka, RabbitMQ). Decoupled, scalable, but harder to debug and trace.

backend.title: How do you handle database migrations in production without downtime?
backend.hint: Backward-compatible migrations, blue-green deploys, feature flags. Expand-contract pattern for column changes.

backend.title: What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling?
backend.hint: Vertical: bigger machine. Horizontal: more machines. Horizontal preferred for high availability but requires stateless services.

backend.title: Explain how Redis works and common use cases.
backend.hint: In-memory key-value store. Use for caching, sessions, pub/sub, rate limiting, leaderboards.

backend.title: How do you handle errors gracefully in a Node.js/Express application?
backend.hint: Global error middleware, async error wrapping (express-async-errors), structured error responses, logging.

system_design.title: Design a URL shortener like bit.ly. Scale it to 100M URLs.
system_design.hint: Base62 encoding, NoSQL for key-value, CDN for redirects, estimate QPS and storage first.

system_design.title: Design YouTube / Netflix video streaming architecture.
system_design.hint: Object storage for videos, CDN distribution, adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS/DASH), separate upload and streaming paths.

system_design.title: Design a notification system that handles 10M notifications per day.
system_design.hint: Message queue (Kafka), separate service per channel (push, email, SMS), fan-out strategy, delivery guarantees.

system_design.title: Design a distributed rate limiter that works across multiple servers.
system_design.hint: Token bucket with Redis. Lua script for atomic operations. Sliding window for fairness.

system_design.title: Design Twitter's feed / timeline system.
system_design.hint: Push vs pull model. Fan-out on write for most users, pull for celebrities. Redis for feed cache.

system_design.title: Design a typeahead / autocomplete search system.
system_design.hint: Trie data structure, precompute top-k suggestions, CDN cache by prefix, debounce on frontend.

system_design.title: Design Google Drive / Dropbox file storage system.
system_design.hint: Block storage for deduplication, metadata in SQL, delta sync, conflict resolution strategy.

system_design.title: Design a real-time chat application like WhatsApp.
system_design.hint: WebSockets for real-time, message queues for delivery guarantees, presence system, read receipts.

system_design.title: Design a ride-sharing service like Uber.
system_design.hint: Geospatial indexing (QuadTree/H3), matching service, driver location updates via WebSocket, surge pricing.

system_design.title: Design a distributed cache like Memcached.
system_design.hint: Consistent hashing for distribution, eviction policies (LRU), replication for availability.

system_design.title: How would you design an API gateway?
system_design.hint: Auth, rate limiting, request routing, load balancing, logging, circuit breaker at the gateway level.

system_design.title: Design a leaderboard system for a game with 50M users.
system_design.hint: Redis sorted sets for real-time rankings, batch updates for historical, pagination with rank offset.

devops.title: Explain the difference between Docker containers and virtual machines.
devops.hint: VMs virtualize hardware (hypervisor). Containers share OS kernel. Containers are faster, lighter, but less isolated.

devops.title: How does Kubernetes manage container orchestration? Explain pods, deployments, and services.
devops.hint: Pod: smallest unit (1+ containers). Deployment: manages pod replicas. Service: stable network endpoint for pods.

devops.title: What is CI/CD? Walk me through setting up a pipeline with GitHub Actions.
devops.hint: Trigger on push/PR → lint/test → build → deploy. Use secrets for credentials, environments for staging/prod.

devops.title: Explain blue-green deployment and canary deployment. When do you use each?
devops.hint: Blue-green: switch entire traffic at once. Canary: gradually shift traffic %. Canary better for risk mitigation.

devops.title: How does Kubernetes handle service discovery and load balancing?
devops.hint: kube-proxy rules, CoreDNS for service name resolution, Service types (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer).

devops.title: What is Infrastructure as Code? Compare Terraform vs CloudFormation.
devops.hint: Declarative config for cloud resources. Terraform: multi-cloud, HCL. CloudFormation: AWS-only, JSON/YAML.

devops.title: Explain how you would set up monitoring and alerting for a production system.
devops.hint: Metrics (Prometheus), logs (ELK/Loki), traces (Jaeger). Alert on SLI breach, define SLOs, avoid alert fatigue.

devops.title: What is a Dockerfile? Explain best practices for writing one.
devops.hint: Minimal base image, multi-stage builds, layer caching, non-root user, .dockerignore, pin versions.

devops.title: How do you handle secrets management in a Kubernetes cluster?
devops.hint: Kubernetes Secrets (base64), external secrets (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager), sealed-secrets for GitOps.

devops.title: What is a service mesh? When would you use Istio or Linkerd?
devops.hint: Sidecar proxies handle mTLS, observability, traffic management without code changes. Use at scale when networking complexity grows.

devops.title: Explain how you'd set up a multi-region deployment for high availability.
devops.hint: Active-active vs active-passive. DNS-based routing (Route 53 health checks), data replication lag, consistency trade-offs.

android.title: Explain the Android Activity lifecycle. How do you handle configuration changes?
android.hint: onCreate → onStart → onResume → onPause → onStop → onDestroy. Use ViewModel to survive config changes.

android.title: What is Jetpack Compose? How is it different from XML-based UI?
android.hint: Declarative UI in Kotlin. Recomposition instead of view inflation. Easier state management, less boilerplate.

android.title: How does Room database work with LiveData and ViewModel?
android.hint: Room: ORM for SQLite. LiveData: observable data holder. ViewModel: survives configuration changes. MVVM pattern.

android.title: Explain Kotlin coroutines and how they differ from threads.
android.hint: Lightweight, non-blocking. Structured concurrency with scopes. Dispatchers: Main, IO, Default. Suspend functions.

android.title: What is the difference between Service, IntentService, and WorkManager?
android.hint: Service: long-running on main thread. IntentService: deprecated, background worker. WorkManager: guaranteed deferred work.

android.title: How does Retrofit work? How do you handle network errors?
android.hint: Type-safe HTTP client. Use Response<T> to handle errors. Interceptors for logging and auth headers.

android.title: Explain Dependency Injection in Android using Hilt.
What is the Android Navigation component and how do you pass data between fragments?
android.hint: Safe Args for type-safe argument passing. NavController, NavGraph, back stack management.

android.title: How do you optimize RecyclerView performance for a large list?
android.hint: DiffUtil for efficient updates, ViewHolder pattern, setHasFixedSize, image loading with Glide/Coil, avoid overdraw.

android.title: Explain ProGuard / R8 and why they matter for Android releases.
android.hint: Code shrinking, obfuscation, optimization. Reduces APK size. Keep rules needed for reflection and serialization.

hr.title: Tell me about yourself. Walk me through your professional journey.
hr.hint: Past → Present → Future structure. Keep to 2 minutes. Focus on relevant experience, not life story.

hr.title: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager. How did you handle it?
hr.hint: STAR format. Show respect + assertiveness. Focus on professional disagreement, not personal conflict.

hr.title: Describe the most challenging technical problem you've solved.
hr.hint: Set up the context (why hard), your thought process, actions taken, measurable outcome.

hr.title: Where do you see yourself in 5 years? How does this role fit your plans?
hr.hint: Show growth ambition, connect to role's progression path. Avoid: CEO answer. Include: skills you want to build.

hr.title: How do you handle working under tight deadlines with multiple priorities?
hr.hint: Mention prioritization framework, stakeholder communication, and a real example.

hr.title: Describe a time you failed. What did you learn from it?
hr.hint: Own the failure clearly, don't deflect blame. The learning and follow-up actions are what matter most.

hr.title: Why are you leaving your current job? / Why do you want this role?
hr.hint: Stay positive about current employer. Focus on growth opportunity, not escape. Research the company's mission.

hr.title: How do you handle feedback and criticism from peers or seniors?
hr.hint: Show openness, give example of implementing feedback, mention how you seek it proactively.

hr.title: Describe a time you had to learn something quickly. How did you approach it?
hr.hint: Learning strategy, resources used, timeline, outcome. Good place to highlight self-teaching ability.

hr.title: How do you collaborate with non-technical team members on projects?
hr.hint: Translate technical concepts to plain language, align on goals, regular check-ins, document decisions.

hr.title: Tell me about a time you showed leadership without formal authority.
hr.hint: Influence through expertise and trust, not title. Show initiative, mention impact on team outcome.

hr.title: What are your greatest strengths and weaknesses?
hr.hint: Strength: genuine with evidence. Weakness: real but not role-critical, show active improvement steps.

aptitude.title: A train travels 360 km at a uniform speed. If the speed had been 5 km/h more, it would have taken 1 hour less. Find the original speed.
aptitude.hint: Use distance = speed × time. Set up equation: 360/s - 360/(s+5) = 1.

aptitude.title: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 12 hours and 15 hours. How long to fill it together?
A bag contains 4 red and 6 blue balls. What is the probability of drawing 2 red balls?
Find the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
A person invests ₹10,000 at 10% per annum compound interest. What is the amount after 2 years?
In how many ways can the letters of the word PROGRAMMING be arranged?
aptitude.hint: Total = 11! / (2! × 2! × 2!) — divide by repeated letters G, R, M. = 4989600.

aptitude.title: A shopkeeper sells an item at 20% profit. If he had bought it at 20% less, profit % would be?
If CLOCK is coded as KCOLC, how is TIGER coded?
aptitude.hint: The word is reversed. TIGER → REGIT.

aptitude.title: A is twice as old as B. 5 years ago, A was 3 times as old as B. Find their current ages.
In a 100m race, A beats B by 10m and B beats C by 10m. By how much does A beat C?
What is the angle between hour and minute hands at 3:40?
aptitude.hint: Hour hand at 3:40 → 110°. Minute hand at 40 min → 240°. Angle = |240-110| = 130°.

aptitude.title: Find the missing term: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ?
aptitude.hint: Fibonacci sequence. Each term = sum of previous two. 8 + 13 = 21.

aptitude.title: A man rows at 8 km/h in still water. River flows at 2 km/h. Time to row 30 km downstream?
If 6 men can do a work in 10 days, how many men are needed to finish in 4 days?
A number when divided by 3 leaves remainder 1, by 4 leaves remainder 2. Find smallest such number.
aptitude.hint: CRT: x ≡ 1 (mod 3), x ≡ 2 (mod 4). Try: 10 ÷ 3 = R1, 10 ÷ 4 = R2. Answer: 10.
